text: Organized and knows how to explain complex topics in a simple way. He's respectful, helpful, and actually wants students to succeed. Highly recommend taking him.

He's a solid professor. You can tell he has experience in the CS workforce and isn't just another boring CS lecturer. He tries to connect the topics with either IRL applications or directly in CS which I appreciate a lot - it really helped me grasp the concept faster. If you take notes and watch the lectures, you should do well in this class.

I took 206&210 w Blackmore. I highly recommend him. He makes content very easy to understand & curves generously, dropping lowest quiz, lowest hw & lower of 2 midterms! Also substitutes SIRS completion % w the last hw. 210 lecture was difficult bc of poor acoustics & a kid who often disrupted the flow. By October 90% of students were not attending.

Most of the material in the course was straightforward and there weren't any trick questions. I didn't like how all of his exams had strict grading criteria. There was no partial credit for answering any questions, so the average score for his exams were pretty low. Thankfully, he curved the class a lot.

He is a really caring professor who always responds to emails and provides many ways to improve your grades. There are 5 projects (lowest dropped), 5-6 quizzes (lowest dropped), 2 midterms (lowest dropped), and a final. Overall, it is a relatively easy course with this professor but get ready to teach yourself everything.
The professor is sweet but the course no matter the teacher is terrible.

His lectures are the most mind-numbing I have ever attended. His teaching skills reflect his 25-year absence from academia. Avoid taking any classes with him; you will gain nothing from them. You would gain more by watching a random Python tutorial on YouTube than by spending a semester attending this man's lectures.
Great Professor! Extremely concise and easy-to-follow lectures + many opportunities to earn points, wants every student to get an A!
Professor Blackmore is a very kind and chill person. He wants you to succeed and gives plenty of opportunity to do so. If you see him for CS classes, please take.

Incredibly generous grader and seemed to be in his element even in online lectures. Lowest quiz and homework grades were dropped and so so so many extra credit opportunities were presented. Please take a class with him when you get the chance

Would 100% guarantee her to anyone else. Very kind on the exams and grading. Cares about you learning the topics well and doing well in her class. Lots of Extra Credit, lenient grading, and she answers quickly on email outside of class.

Burns is definitely the professor you'd want to take for 211. I had her F24, and she made this class incredibly straightforward. There are also many extra credit opportunities, and compared to some of the other professors teaching this class, she reduces the stress tremendously.

Took Prof. Burns in F24 and if you're lucky enough to have her, congrats! Homeworks are a lil difficult but there's a lot of extra credit opportunities that make the class extremely hard to fail or do poorly. Exams are straightforward. Random attendance checks but it's worth going because she actually makes the material enjoyable. 5/5 definitely.

Easy professor for CompArch, lots and lots of extra credit opportunities, quizzes and tests were straightforward and pretty easy if you pay attention and look over the lecture slides. Attendance is taken randomly. She's very nice and kind, definitely recommend for 211.

F24 She was very understanding and gave a lot of extra credit. The class is so much better with her, she is super understanding and gave extensions to multiple assignments. Took feedback and responded. Assignments were also not super difficult, she was also very engaging during class.

Super nice. Lectures were engaging. She took attendance randomly. If you start all of the projects early, it's an easy A (don't do them the day before like I did). Bomb lab was super fun, don't be intimidated by it. There's really great YT tutorials you can follow. Take-home exams(initially in-person). Oral exam was graded super leniently.

F24 was her first non-summer session teaching at Rutgers and Prof Burns was great. Flexible with due dates and the assignments weren't impossibly difficult. She also gave a ton of extra credit so it was basically impossible to get a B. She was also j really nice and pleasant which was a plus.
Outstanding professor, goes out of his way to help his students succeed.

I would say his Lectures were amazing. Apparently, he had personal issues, but it took up so much of my energy and anxiety with this class, as he never graded anything!! The HW was bad due to how it's worded (same for the exams), but felt quite lenient b/c of that. I got most of my grades the DAY OF the final. Final Grade appeared on the 6th

Think he had an off semester because grading took a long time, but lectures are engaging and he's willing to work with students. Disorganized grading and homework is hard but fair, and he gives a lot of time for it. Exams weren't bad. Put in effort and its easy to do well in this course.

Unfortunately, class with this prof took years off my life. Lacked structure and expectations, particularly with grading. Almost impossible to get in touch with him, and homework was impossible. No topics list or exam guidance was provided, A minimal amount of organization and transparency would have made all the difference.

A passionate lecturer and easy to follow (with many examples too). I really enjoyed his lectures. Assignments were hard but doable, and helped me learn. Inaccessible outside of class and took forever to grade things (though he did mention some personal issues he was going through at the time). If you can get a hold of him, he's a great resource.

Off semester because of personal stuff so really slow grading. HW's were hard but doable. Exams were very tough but graded fairly leniently (70 avg~ for both). 25% per exam, 50% hw (3-4 a sem, we had 3). Great lecturer but notes are hard to follow if you don't go to class. Use the textbook. Overall really nice guy and willing to work with you.

He writes notes on blank sheets, which I found engaging. He welcomes questions and discussion during lecture. All 3 projects take time, but he gives enough time as long as you pace yourself. His personal life likely impacted his grading schedule. Strangely, he tends to open the submission box late, and he did not answer e-mails this semester.

This professor is very disorganized. Emails were never answered, which made communication difficult. We did not have proper practice material for exams, and there was little guidance on how to study. Until mid-winter break hw and exam grades were incomplete, so many students didnt know where they stood. This caused confusion about expectations.

The other ratings saying he can be disorganized are true, but I think he really cares and tries to help you out. If you put in effort you will pass, don't let the negative reviews scare you. Personally, as long as I pass I'm happy - and this professor will certainly help you achieve that.

Should clarify that the professor had personal issues, which contributes to this rating. His lectures are great, you learn a lot from them. Unfortunately, class was horribly disorganized. Any other semester I think the class would be run far better, but not this semester unfortunately.

Bro never post grade. The semester is finish and I only got my grade for hw1. He had posted the grades for hw2, but he immediately withdrew them. I have no idea what he's up to. Notes are handwriting and exams are difficult. No practice exams and there are few example problems. Everything is in the announcement and get everything posted very late.

He had some personal issues this semester, which might have impacted performance. With that being said, HW portals are not posted until 8-10 PM the day of (11:59 due) and grades take weeks to release (midterm grade still not out yet & just finished final). Difficult notes & often posted late. Doesn't respond to emails. But good teacher & nice guy.

This teacher is absolutely AMAZING! He deeply cares about his students and wants them to do well. However, this class is EXTREMELY DIFFICULT with this teacher. I would not recommend it to anyone unless they are willing to put in multiple hours each week of dedicated studying to understand the content.

One of the best professors at Rutgers, if not the world. He actually cares about student understanding. Really easy to get in touch with outside of class. Homework is really hard though, but he is happy to help out.

This was a challenging course, but the projects really solidified my understanding of the material. While the assignments were time-consuming, using resources like unstuckstudy alongside the lectures helped me manage the workload. The professor was accessible and provided good support.

Best CS professor you could take. Class can be somewhat challenging if you arent a CS major or don't have experience, but she is always willing to help. Just take advantage of meeting with her and asking questions. Quiz/Tests are mostly on what you learn in class and a bit of slide material. Focus on coding and things she repeats for a good grade.
Like the ratings say; she's helpful, and clear, which in turn makes the work easy (even though it does require some thinking).
I learned so much in this class. She's super! Very helpful. Makes the most ambiguous stuff quite understandable.

Really intimidating at first, but once you get to know that she won't bite your head off, she's approachable and pretty nice. Open to questions. Makes time for students. Gets programming languages confused at times, though. Overall pretty good.
Great professor! Cares if you understand the material and doesn't mind questions.

The way he teaches is that he moves very quickly regardless of whether anyone understands what he's saying and his accent makes that even harder. There is no canvas page only the homework assignments and lecture notes to prepare for the four tests that make up most of your grade. By far the worst instructor I've ever had.

No Canvas, no textbook, how to understand the his talking, ugly handwriting, poor communication. bad tests.(He wants to discourage his students to study.- After the first exam, I didn't go his class only taking exams. I got B+ without final exam but I wanted to study seriously but the class was waste time and waste money. Avoid his classes.
though he is tough grader, his lectures are the only lectures I look forward to all week! amazing amazing professor!
Bad teacher. Don't understand the hype around him but he is straight up not a good teacher--he's not good at teaching at all.

I'm not finished the course yet, but I will say he does not teach in a way that is easy for students to learn from. If you're coming into the class expecting a compassionate and patient professor who will walk through and explain a problem thoroughly for you, you are sadly mistaken. Be prepared to have to teach yourself.
I'm not even completed with the class yet, but compared
his course is challenging but he knows how to make you successful in the field.

The fundamental issue with classes he teaches is that he does not understand "how" students learn. His teaching methodology consists of writing examples on a white board without first explaining the problems presented. In other words, there is no "teaching" that occurs, he never shows you how to do the problems, he just goes through them. Avoid.

Worst Professor/Class I've ever taken. Could barely understand his lecture and his notes. 8am class. Went over difficult topics way to quick. Would belittle students if they didn't understand what he was teaching. Class AVG for the first test was a 30 which is totally unacceptable. Very rude and unapproachable. Do not take him.

Many college courses are too easy and it does set students up for failure after they graduate. There is a ton of competition in the software field and getting a degree doesn't guarantee you a good position anywhere. Professor Ghandi is no different than any teacher that wants their students to do more than what is expected.

Just save yourself the trouble and avoid him if you can. His math foundations class started with around 50-60 people and had less than 10 left when I withdrew. Confusing when explaining concepts, will put you on the spot daily, and extremely hard grader. If your final answer is wrong good luck trying to get partial credit.
I would go for somebody else if I can. He sounds very fishy at times.

Professor Gandhi is amazing. He can be very rough, but he is only asking you to use your brain and solve problems. That is what is necessary for a job in the field. I never saw him take attendance, but if you miss a class, you will regret it. He does put fake solutions up, but he always told us that it was wrong and why. You will learn A LOT.

The hardest professor I've had, bar none. He's tough. His lectures move quick and he can be harsh at times. But Rajiv Gandhi is the most rewarding professor I have ever had. If you put the effort in, you will walk away from this class with the knowledge and confidence you need to succeed. If you put the effort in, he'll pass you. Great professor.

Math found is learning discrete math, a concept that is pretty hard. He will make you work for your grade and assigns the homework for you to do as more practice. It is kind of hard to understand when he's teaching difficult concepts, but make sure to put in A LOT of effort in this class. I guarantee you will be stressed...
She is a very good teacher and is very helpful. Her teaching style is easy to follow. The homework in the cource is a bit hard but rest of it is easy.
Took her for CS111, now she's the only professor I would change my schedule to take her class for CS112. She understands students and makes things easy to understand and teaches very well.
Went to most lectures. She was very caring and helped answer all questions with compassion and alos had interesting projects.

Prof Goel is an excellent professor who's caring and thoughtful in lectures. She explains concepts clearly and asks questions to highlight mistakes. She reviews exams, but I recommend self-study since you'll rely on lectures and recitation problems. Practice often and don't hesitate to seek help. Note: CS profs can't help with coding assignments.
The best CS professor in the department take her class immediately if its open. Great lecturer, always makes sure to go over topics multiple times for student understanding.
Very friendly professor. 111 is a very big course she will make sure to answer questions. Though I do wish she did more practice coding in lecture but she does it relatively well enough.
If given a choice, trust me just blindly register for her class. The best professor to teach CS111 and CS112 out there.
She was a really good lecturer for this course and was very fair.
Professor Goel is one of the best teachers you can ever get. She is so experienced and tends to help you whenever you need it.

Professor Goel is one of the best professors at Rutgers. She gives good lectures where she outlines what to expect on the exams and assignments. She is nice, caring, and understanding toward her students. The assignments and exams were straightforward and it is possible to get an A.

Had her for intro to cs and data structures. Same grade in both, provides great real life examples during class and carefully goes over each topic thoroughly. Very kind and understanding, which is important for CS. Codes during class, wish it was a bit more frequent but amazing professor overall.

Professor Goel is so amazing! Her lectures are easy to follow, and she is good at simplifying complex concepts. I highly recommend her to students trying out computer science for the first time. Even though I already had some computer science experience beforehand, her lectures taught me much more about stuff I already knew.
amazing lecturer, extremely patient and explains topics well. you are lucky if you have her, easy 10/10
She's really nice and ready to help her students. If you go to lecture and keep up with her and her material, you'll be fine!
liked how the course allowed you to make up your grade if you messed up on an exam as the hws really helped raise grades and personally I learned more through the hws than exams.
You can tell he is genuinely a nice person, but unfortunately his lectures aren't the most helpful and the class isn't the most organized.

really liked everything I learned. This was the first class where I really used Python, and it was really exciting to see how effective the language is in data cleaning and processing. In addition, I knew very little about machine learning, but now I'm confident in my skills, especially after the final project. The class was always exciting.

Really like him, made lecture enjoyable. Offered career opportunities and was a lovely person to learn from. Class assignments were manageable. Easily recommend this professor, he makes this easy class fun to be a part of.

He will make you enjoy data science and coding. Though he is a little disorganized and uncoordinated, he is excellent at teaching concepts. He always patiently answers all student questions as well. Definitely try to take him for CS210.

although he is disorganized, he is a very nice professor and is also enthusiastic about lectures. class is project heavy with 4 assignments (30%), 5 quizzes (20%), participation (10%), midterm1 (10%), midterm2 (15%), and final project (15%). there is no final exam but the final project is comprehensive.

I thoroughly enjoyed having him as a professor for this class. I feel like I've learnt so much useful information and am definitely now considering data analysis as a future career path! I've even registered for other data science courses that align with the major because of how much I've come to appreciate data management this semester.
The shining star of the Rutgers CS Department.

prof is Enthusiastic, cheerful, and approachable. He encouraged curiosity, linked lessons to real-world use cases, and supported student learning with extra resources (e.g., Cubits videos). I liked the class.

He teaches alongside an AI, which would be fine if he actually knew what he was teaching. He seems to not prepare for his lectures, and changes his exam dates several times in the week leading up to the exam. His assignments are reasonable, and he grades reasonably, but it's his way or the highway, and nothing is his fault. Don't recommend.
He's a great teacher. The class was enjoyable and getting a good grade was very doable.
Chill guy, chill class. Would take again

Guna is fine, but I wish he coordinated with his TAs more, since they had a lot of conflicting information. I don't like his required attendance policy, its all very unclear too. I probably could have gotten an A if I bothered to show up more. Final project was cool though, I liked how he let you go wild as long as you fulfilled his requirements.

Attendance required but easy class if you do the work. 4-5 Labs:35%, 5 Quizzes:20%, Midterm: 20%, Course Project:15%, Attendance:10%. Project seems tough since you have to come up with your idea for what to do but they are easy on the grading. Recitation attendance also required. Uploads lecture slides online. Does a curve based on everyone's grade

He's a good lecturer and fair grader. Genuinely cares about his students learning, and his lectures reflect that. That being said he is very disorganized and does not give clear criteria on how he will grade you on assignments and even for the class, so be prepared to just guess on a lot of things. However he is very approachable and understanding.

Dr. Kulikowski reminisces his work in the 1970s and rambles about it ad nauseam. The midterm was an oral exam, but he grades leniently. However, it is hard to say what I actually learned in the course. Propose a halfway interesting project idea with a halfway decent execution and presentation to do well in the course.
easy A but don't take him if you actually want to learn something
The class is graded on 2 assignments, 2 quizzes, 1 project, and 2 exams. The exams have almost no material to study and also the professor will put stuff not covered in class on the exam.

Might be the worst prof I have ever taken. Extremely disorganized, lectures were useless. Recitation was where you learned anything. He had no clue where the class was going and randomly one night at 1am dropped an announcement we had our final in a week. Do not take this professor.

Worst CS class ever taken. Man just rambles on while looking at one slide and expects us to know many things about other course despite the pre-req for 440. Lectures give no information, and the slides are 50-90 slides long with confusing information.

He never communicates, didn't give us any grades, gave ppl wrong grades from the same group with the same submissions(literally the exact same file), never responds to emails, never got feedback, his exam had questions and topics that he didn't cover, not a good teacher, and he changes the course structure without telling us anything.
bro just puts up slides and rants about this conference that he did in 1975 about Ai. no actual lecturing. learned everything by self-studying. super disorganized.

I won't be surprised if the only grade we got back was the final grade on our transcript. It seems like he doesn't have a proper plan for this class and is making up the syllabus as we go. His lectures have no substance, and the recitations are where you learn the actual content.

This class was the most disorganized class I had to take at Rutgers. Our final exam date was announced at 1AM one week before and it's in the middle of April instead of finals week!!! Now, we have no idea what the grade distribution will be. We are near the end of the semester and absolutely nothing has been graded & no date when they'll be posted.

The professor was so hilarious and funny! He accidentally sat down on the chair and almost fell down! The professor gave us books about machine learning. However, the quizzes were very hard and lot of writing, so I tried to use ChatGPT and answered questions. The oral exam was pretty easy, and the professor was helpful with my answers.

The course is very disorganized, and he never tells you what's happening in class, and won't make any announcements. He doesn't really teach the topics well, have to self study all of the topics. If you are taking his course, you have to attend his lectures and recitations to even know what's happening.

Took a Byrne Seminar on AI and ChatGPT, but it turned into a confusing philosophy lesson. The discussions felt like ramblings on unrelated topics. Despite the boredom, the course was a Pass/Fail, and I passed. Not what I expected, though. He seems to be a nice person, but I do not recommend him for any CS courses.

Simply put: do not take him. I worked hard in this class but he is a horrible professor. He turns all lessons into a philosophy lesson rather than teaching us what actually matters. You'll end up teaching yourself which is what I ended up doing and I still did not pass. Seriously ridiculous we pay for teachers who can't perform their jobs properly.
I mean he was not that bad. As long as you studied, went to lectures, and completed all the quizzes you should end up with a fairly decent grade.

Professor Kulikowski is a great professor, but he is horrible at lectures. Worst CS major lectures ever. Literally learned almost nothing from lectures. Homework and quizzes are hard. Do not take his section trust me.

He's AWFUL! He didn't teach anything during lectures; just went on about random stuff trying to be inspirational, didn't teach anything! Learned everything on my own. He didn't even cover course material we were supposed to cover like graph theory. Quizzes/tests were really hard; all he talked about was his research being an inspiration to us.

I've had Menny for 211, 214, and 314. He has tough exams and projects but very generous curving that more then makes up for it. I personally never attended classes and performed very well in all 3 courses. As long as you put in the work you will get the grade. He is very nice and knowledgeable about the material. I would take him again!
Best. Professor. Ever. So knowledgeable and great at teaching. His lectures are awesome, and he always answers questions thoroughly. Class is somewhat hard, but reasonable if you come to lecture.

Monotonous lectures, but he's down-to-earth and accessible. Homeworks are very difficult, so be sure to do well on them or else you won't pass. Tests are also difficult but they are mainly conceptual, trivia-type questions with little-no coding. Also, the curve is negligible, so don't rely on it and do the best you can.
I did not do well on exams and only passed because of projects. So, try your best at every project and also do well on exams.

poor teaching skills, cannot keep students engaged in lecture, and focuses on teaching info that is irrelevant on exams, HW, and in careers. review session before exam is helpful tho. recitations were very disorganized; none of my classmates attended. final was very fair & reasonable.

David Menendez is a RACIST. he said "ik its tight in here, hopefully you all showered" now MIND YOU this is the most indian dense state, and the subject is statistically indian majoritied (computer science). I thought this was unprofessional. he posted the review questions for the final a day before, contains wrong answers (watch out for 4b-e)!!!!!

I have had Menendez for two classes now, and I can confidently say that you need to stay focused on the assignments. The assignments are very important, and doing badly on them means you will not pass. The tests are not particularly difficult and more closely resemble trivia exams than coding exams, as they are filled with random questions.
Simply underrated. If you can't read your assignment requirements stop complaining.

Menendez is very dry and pretty by the books, but he is very knowledgeable about computer architecture and went through examples multiple times for students who were confused. His class is hard and the projects MUST be started early if you want a good grade on them, but its doable and manageable. Tests are not heavy on coding, more regular problems

There are 3-5 projects and the rest of the grade is exams. Exams are very hard. Projects are very hard to do right, and perfection is pretty much required to pass the class. Hard cutoffs, had to retake class for a 69.5%. Unhelpful lectures. Unhelpful Canvas. No updates on Canvas, and VERY slow grading.

The class is not as bad as Algos, but it's not great, especially that one project. Most people get to phase 3 or 6 of it, but I finished, and it took ALL my time for two full weeks. Exams are hard, but doable. Projects are hard, some not doable. This professor's lectures are not very helpful. And speaking to him outside of class also isn't helpful.

made me hate coding in C, made me hate everything computer architecture. please save yourself and don't take him if you're someone who's dependent on a teacher to pass a class. i passed but i fought for my life to

Prof. Menendez is a very good teacher; his explanations of C and low-level architecture are thorough, cohesive and delivered in perfect English!!! (rare for Rutgers). The midterm and final exams include code-on-paper, so you'll need to put in real effort to do well, but his class is absolutely worth it.

The lectures were dry, but luckily everything gets posted online so you can just skip pretty comfortably. Recitation optional as well. 50% projects 50% midterm/final. Absolutely 0 respect for students' time. Assigned projects without giving enough time on occasion. Class averages were very high with fair exams though, so overall the A is free.

Insanely disorganized class. Lectures weren't helpful at all and felt like a fun facts session more than an actual lecture (coming from someone that listened and took notes every class.) Projects and exams required self study from start to finish but were doable. Did not give enough time for the projects for the amount of work required in them.

I actually enjoyed his class. He also really tried to ensure that students learned the algorithms properly. You need to read the textbook and go to lecture. Also, he holds review sessions during his office hours and his practice exams are very similar to the actual exams. He's also brilliant in his field so it was cool to have him

Barely came to class (including the final exam), made "creative problems" on the exams even though he never even came to teach. Luckily, attendance was not mandatory, so better to self study or take another professor.

Bottom 3 profs in this department imo. Dull, uninteresting, practically a ghost when trying to reach out. Also, prob the worst final grade distribution ive ever seen. Final is worth 40%, so no matter what you get (with the exception of an A+) be prepared to witness your final grade fall some letter grades at the end of the semester.

Difficult to follow and genuinely does not care at all. He was abcsent for half the semester and didn't even bother showing up for the final. Makes the final weighted at 40% and gives the most stingy curve if you can even call it that? Avoid and take Zhang instead

Really unreliable professor. He is not accessible even in the slightest outside of class. He missed the first month of classes because he was busy working in another country. Aside from that, his lectures are okay, and homework isn't too bad. But I wouldn't recommend him to anyone.

Horrible class. Honestly horrible teacher. His exams are so hard, people complained to the dean and they said they couldn't do anything because he's famous. He sings in Hungarian during exams. Sometimes lets his daughter teach the class (and she is better lol). Very disorganized content. Do not recommend professor.

This class was a disaster. He openly admitted to being a terrible teacher + not wanting to teach, and even let his daughter take over the class at times. If you want to get anything out of this class, please for the love of god take it with someone else (I would say it's worth pushing it back a semester if Mario is the only option). Easy A tho tbf.

I attended every lecture, but found that I learned more just from reading the textbook (and could learn the same topic in half the time). He did things slowly and we were far behind the other prof's sections of 344. He made interesting algorithms boring. Granted, he is caring and tried to learn many people's names. But take a diff prof if you can.
I like the way you walk, the way that you talk I like the way you lecture I like the way you grade
lectures were a mess. unclear grading. no curve even though i was above upper quartile on every assignment and exam.

Professor explains concepts, mentions there is a curve in the beginning of the class but when grades come out there is no curve. Often mentions this course is easy but in reality there are some fundamentals to go through and needed to be explained properly. Disorganised and not a good experience overall.

Nice dude, kinda funny. Class is very not good. No hate to the dude, but going to his lectures and trying to listen to them was like trying to figure out a maze. First day of lecture, he jumped straight into a random topic, and didn't explain the relevance of it. Super disorganized way of teaching. Grades kinda lightly though. Don't take.
Prof. Szegedy taught advanced concepts in algorithms and absolutely killed it. Lectures were heavy and homeworks took time, bit it was worth it.
Exams are easy. Very little theory. Course was geared towards passing interviews.

Nice guy, but didnt seem to care about the class, and as whole it was very unorganized; it wasnt always clear what he expected from you, which made it hard to prepare for exams even though they were open book. Told us things at the last possible minute. But he does his best to ensure that you pass. Brilliant researcher, but not the best professor
